title: MAESTRO
logline: A biographical portrait of Leonard Bernstein's life and his complex relationship with his wife Felicia Montealegre, exploring his rise as a conductor and composer while navigating his identity, sexuality, and devotion to his family.
antagonistOrConflict: Internal conflict between Bernstein's public success, private sexuality, and commitment to his marriage with Felicia

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Character-driven three-act structure following the arc of Leonard and Felicia's relationship from meeting through courtship, marriage and family life, to illness and death
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Opens with framing device of older Leonard, then moves chronologically through their relationship with increasing emotional intensity. Pacing accelerates in the final act as Felicia's illness progresses.
